Platform

User

Growth

Problem

Statement

EdTech

platforms

operate

in

a

highly

competitive

and

rapidly

evolving

digital

environment.

To

ensure

sustainable

growth,

companies

must

anticipate

user

acquisition

trends,

engagement

levels,

and

potential

drop-offs.

The

objective

of

this

project

is

to

analyze

student

enrollment

and

engagement

data

to

estimate

Monthly

Active

Users

(MAU)

and

forecast

future

platform

growth.

By

examining

activity

patterns,

dropout

probability,

and

course

participation

trends,

we

aim

to

identify

growth

drivers,

retention

risks,

and

actionable

business

insights

that

can

support

strategic

planning

for

content

development,

marketing,

and

infrastructure

expansion.

Dataset

Source

and

Justification

Dataset

Name:

Student

Drop-off

Prediction

in

EdTech

Courses

Source:

Kaggle

Link:

https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/vandanrana/student-drop-off-prediction-in-edtech-courses

Total

Records:

40,000

students

This

dataset

was

selected

because

it

contains

comprehensive

behavioral,

engagement,

and

demographic

information

about

students

enrolled

in

online

courses.

Although

the

dataset

was

originally

designed

to

predict

student

drop-offs,

it

includes

key

indicators

such

as

enrollment

dates,

last

active

dates,

engagement

scores,

attendance

rates,

and

dropout

probabilities.

These

variables

make

it

highly

suitable

for

estimating

active

users

and

forecasting

platform

growth

trends.

The

dataset

provides

sufficient

historical

depth

(2020–2025)

and

scale

(40,000

users),

allowing

meaningful

analysis

of

enrollment

patterns,

engagement

behavior,

and

churn

risk.

Dataset

Description

Each

row

in

the

dataset

represents

one

student

enrolled

in

nt

historical

depth

(2020–2025)

and

scale

(40,000

users),

allowing

meaningful

analysis

of

enrollment

patterns,

engagement

behavior,

and

churn

risk.

Dataset

Description

Each

row

in

the

dataset

represents

one

student

enrolled

in

an

online

course.

The

dataset

includes

demographic

information,

academic

performance

indicators,

engagement

metrics,

and

risk

classification

variables.

Key

Variables

Used

in

This

Project:

●

Student_Id

–

Unique

identifier

for

each

student

●

Age,

Gender,

Country

–

Demographic

attributes

●

Education_Level

&

Employment_Status

–

Background

details

●

Course_Name

&

Course_Category

–

Type

of

course

enrolled

●

Course_Fee

–

Revenue-related

variable

●

Enrollment_Date

–

Used

to

analyze

user

acquisition

trends

●

Last_Active_Date

–

Used

to

estimate

active

users

●

Engagement_Score

(0–1)

–

Measures

overall

student

engagement

●

Progress_Percent

–

Course

completion

percentage

●

Attendance_Rate

&

Assignment_Submission_Rate

–

Participation

indicators

●

Dropout_Probability

&

Dropout_Status

–

Churn

risk

indicators

●

Dropout_Risk_Level

–

Categorized

risk

(Low,

Medium,

High)

●

Burnout_Indicator

–

Identifies

potential

disengagement

These

variables

collectively

help

approximate

Monthly

Active

Users,

identify

retention

risks,

and

analyze

growth

patterns

across

different

segments.

Data

Audit

Summary

Dataset

Size

●

Total

Students:

40,000

●

Time

Period

Covered:

2020–2025

Enrollment

Distribution

by

Year

Enrollment

remained

relatively

stable

between

2020

and

2024,

averaging

approximately

7,400–7,500

new

users

annually.

The

lower

number

in

2025

reflects

partial-year

data.

Country

Distribution

●

India

contributes

approximately

50%

of

total

users.

●

Other

significant

markets

include

the

USA,

UK,

Canada,

and

Australia.

●

The

dataset

shows

strong

geographic

concentration

in

India.

Course

ar

data.

Country

Distribution

●

India

contributes

approximately

50%

of

total

users.

●

Other

significant

markets

include

the

USA,

UK,

Canada,

and

Australia.

●

The

dataset

shows

strong

geographic

concentration

in

India.

Course

Category

Distribution

●

AI

&

Data

Science

has

the

highest

enrollment.

●

Finance,

Marketing,

and

HR

follow

closely.

●

Operations

has

comparatively

lower

enrollment.

This

indicates

strong

demand

for

technical

and

analytics-based

programs.

Engagement

and

Performance

Overview

●

Average

Engagement

Score:

~50%

●

Average

Attendance

Rate:

~70%

●

Average

Assignment

Submission

Rate:

~50%

●

Average

Dropout

Probability:

~44%

Risk

Distribution

●

Low

Risk:

15,434

students

●

Medium

Risk:

18,943

students

●

High

Risk:

5,623

students

A

significant

portion

of

students

fall

under

medium

risk,

suggesting

strong

opportunities

for

targeted

retention

strategies.

Data

Cleaning

and

Transformation

Steps

To

ensure

accurate

analysis

and

forecasting,

the

dataset

underwent

systematic

data

cleaning

and

transformation

using

Google

Sheets

formulas.

Handling

Missing

Values

(Categorical

Data)

Blank

values

in

categorical

columns

such

as

Age,

Gender,

Country,

Education

Level,

Employment

Status,

and

Course

Category

were

replaced

with

"NA"

to

maintain

consistency

and

prevent

errors

in

pivot

tables

and

dashboards.

Example

formula

used:

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(ISBLANK(Raw_Data!K2:K40001),"NA",Raw_Data!K2:K40001))

This

ensured

that

no

blank

categorical

values

remained

in

the

cleaned

dataset.

Handling

Missing

Values

(Numerical

Data)

For

numerical

fields,

statistical

imputation

techniques

were

applied

instead

of

replacing

values

with

"NA".

Engagement

Score

–

Median

Imputation

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(ISBLANK(Raw_Data!N2:N40001),

MEDIAN(Raw_Data!N2:N40001),

Raw_Data!N2:N40001))

This

replaced

missing

engagement

values

with

iques

were

applied

instead

of

replacing

values

with

"NA".

Engagement

Score

–

Median

Imputation

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(ISBLANK(Raw_Data!N2:N40001),

MEDIAN(Raw_Data!N2:N40001),

Raw_Data!N2:N40001))

This

replaced

missing

engagement

values

with

the

median

to

reduce

the

effect

of

outliers.

Progress

Percent

–

Average

Imputation

and

Standardization

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(ISBLANK(Raw_Data!O2:O40001),

FLOOR(AVERAGE(Raw_Data!O2:O40001))/100,

Raw_Data!O2:O40001/100))

Missing

progress

values

were

replaced

with

the

average,

and

all

percentage

values

were

standardized

to

decimal

format.

Active

Days

Calculation

The

number

of

active

days

between

enrollment

and

last

activity

was

calculated

using:

=IF(OR(M2="NA",N2="NA",M2="",N2=""),

"NA",

IF(N2>=M2,DATEDIF(M2,N2,"D"),0))

This

helped

approximate

user

activity

duration

for

growth

forecasting.

Age

Group

Categorization

Students

were

segmented

into

meaningful

age

groups

for

demographic

analysis:

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(B2:B40001="NA","NA",

IF(B2:B40001<20,"Teen",

IF(B2:B40001<30,"Young

Adult",

IF(B2:B40001<45,"Adult","Senior")))))

This

transformation

enabled

structured

age-based

analysis

in

pivot

tables.

Performance

Index

Calculation

A

composite

performance

index

was

created

using

equal

weight

(25%)

from

four

key

engagement

metrics:

●

Engagement

Score

●

Attendance

Rate

●

Assignment

Submission

Rate

●

Progress

Percent

Formula

used:

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(K2:K40001="","",

ROUND(

K2:K40001*0.25

+

P2:P40001*0.25

+

O2:O40001*0.25

+

(L2:L40001/100)*0.25

,2)))

This

generated

a

standardized

performance

score

between

0

and

1.

Formula

Implementation

The

following

key

formulas

were

implemented

to

transform

raw

data

into

analytical

variables

used

in

pivot

tables

and

dashboard

development.

Missing

Value

Replacement

(Categorical)

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(ISBLANK(Raw_Data!K2:K40001),"NA",Raw_Data!K2:K40001))

Purpose:

Replace

blank

nted

to

transform

raw

data

into

analytical

variables

used

in

pivot

tables

and

dashboard

development.

Missing

Value

Replacement

(Categorical)

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(ISBLANK(Raw_Data!K2:K40001),"NA",Raw_Data!K2:K40001))

Purpose:

Replace

blank

categorical

entries

with

"NA".

Median

Imputation

(Engagement

Score)

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(ISBLANK(Raw_Data!N2:N40001),

MEDIAN(Raw_Data!N2:N40001),

Raw_Data!N2:N40001))

Purpose:

Maintain

distribution

stability

while

filling

missing

numeric

values.

Percentage

Standardization

(Progress

Percent)

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(ISBLANK(Raw_Data!O2:O40001),

FLOOR(AVERAGE(Raw_Data!O2:O40001))/100,

Raw_Data!O2:O40001/100))

Purpose:

Convert

percentage

values

into

standardized

decimal

format.

Active

Days

Calculation

=IF(OR(M2="NA",N2="NA",M2="",N2=""),

"NA",

IF(N2>=M2,DATEDIF(M2,N2,"D"),0))

Purpose:

Estimate

total

activity

duration

per

student.

Age

Group

Segmentation

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(B2:B40001="NA","NA",

IF(B2:B40001<20,"Teen",

IF(B2:B40001<30,"Young

Adult",

IF(B2:B40001<45,"Adult","Senior")))))

Purpose:

Enable

demographic

segmentation

analysis.

Performance

Index

Formula

=ARRAYFORMULA(IF(K2:K40001="","",

ROUND(

K2:K40001*0.25

+

P2:P40001*0.25

+

O2:O40001*0.25

+

(L2:L40001/100)*0.25

,2)))

Purpose:

Generate

an

overall

performance

score

combining

engagement

and

progress

metrics.
